tldr: Moving from Datadog to Elastic trades proprietary agents and per-host pricing for the open ELK stack — more setup, more control, often lower long-run cost. The pivot point is whether your team will operate Elasticsearch (self-hosted) or rely on Elastic Cloud.
why_switch: Self-host option keeps data in your infra
export_method: Use Datadog's public APIs and the official Terraform provider to capture monitors, dashboards, SLOs, and synthetic configs as code. Pull historical logs via the logs query API in paginated batches and save as NDJSON. Pull metrics via the metrics query API into Prometheus-style or CSV formats. Export users/teams through the account API. For APM traces, recent windows can be queried via the API; older traces may not be retrievable after retention. Stage everything in object storage so the new platform can ingest at its own pace and you have an immutable archive separate from Datadog.
import_method: Set up Elasticsearch (self-hosted via official distributions, or managed via Elastic Cloud) plus Kibana. Switch instrumentation to the Elastic Agent or to OpenTelemetry collectors exporting to Elastic's APM/logs ingest. For logs, point Filebeat/Fluent Bit/Vector at the Elasticsearch ingest endpoints; for metrics, use Metricbeat or OTel. Define index lifecycle policies before bulk-loading historical archives so you control retention and storage tiers. Recreate dashboards in Kibana — query languages differ, so manual rebuild is realistic. Use Kibana's alerting framework to recreate Datadog monitors. Run parallel for 1-2 weeks and validate alert parity and dashboard signal-to-noise.

concept_mapping.note: Choose one ingest stack and standardize

gotchas: Self-hosted Elasticsearch needs ongoing capacity, sharding, and upgrade ops — budget for SRE time
Elastic License 2.0 is not OSI open-source; review legal implications for your use case
Index mapping and shard sizing decisions are hard to undo later — design before bulk import
Dashboard rebuild dominates the migration timeline; prioritize the top 10 used dashboards first
Custom metric cardinality that fit Datadog may need re-modeling for Elasticsearch fields
Run dual ingestion long enough to verify alert parity; cutting Datadog early risks blind spots

email.subject: Data export request and account sunset — please confirm timeline
email.body:
Hi Datadog team,

We are migrating to Elastic and need a full export of our account data in machine-readable formats (JSON, NDJSON, CSV). Required: monitors and alerts, dashboards, synthetic configs, logs within current retention, custom metric series, APM trace samples within retention, users/teams/roles, tag metadata, and notebooks/runbooks.

We are targeting a 14-day export and validation window. Please confirm:

1. Which exports are self-serve via API vs. require a support ticket.
2. Whether log and metric history can be staged to our S3 bucket before cancellation.
3. Billing implications of scaling host counts down during dual-run and the exact contract sunset date once we cancel.

Thanks,
Your customer
